content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239

challenge.lean
summary.md

1	# References
2	
3	<!-- archon:references-summary -->
4	
5	| File | Description |
6	| ---- | ----------- |
7	| `challenge.lean` | Original AI challenge file by Christian Merten — the formal statement of the missing definitions and theorems for the Jacobian of an algebraic curve. The Lean skeleton in `AlgebraicJacobian/` is a decomposition of this file; signatures here are authoritative. |
8

Now I have a clear picture. Let me make the four required edits.

**Edit 1**: Replace the M1.b cofinality proof skeleton in `lem:appLE_isLocalization`.

input.new_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
  The proof decomposes into four steps that together identify $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ with the localization $A_M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$.

  \emph{Step 1: cofinality of basic opens.} The directed system $\{W \subseteq S : f V \subseteq W \subseteq U\}$ of opens between $f V$ and $U$ admits as a cofinal subfamily the family of basic opens
  \[
    \{D(g) \subseteq U : g \in A,\ f V \subseteq D(g)\} \;=\; \{D(g) : g \in M\}.
  \]
  Cofinality holds because: (i) every open $W \subseteq U$ of the affine scheme $U = \Spec A$ is a union of basic opens $D(g_i)$ (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Spec}, via \texttt{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens} or equivalently \texttt{Opens.exists\_finset\_inf\_le\_basicOpen}); (ii) for any $W$ with $f V \subseteq W \subseteq U$, the topological inclusion $f V \subseteq W = \bigcup_i D(g_i)$ combined with quasi-compactness of $f V$ (the image of the quasi-compact affine $V \subseteq X$ under the continuous map $f$, contained in finitely many of the $D(g_i)$) selects a finite sub-cover $f V \subseteq D(g_{i_1}) \cup \cdots \cup D(g_{i_n})$; (iii) for the product $g := g_{i_1} \cdots g_{i_n}$ one has $D(g) = D(g_{i_1}) \cap \cdots \cap D(g_{i_n})$, hence $D(g) \subseteq D(g_{i_j}) \subseteq W$ for each $j$ and $f V \subseteq D(g)$, witnessing the cofinal refinement.

  The bridge identity is
  \[
    f V \subseteq D(g) \;\iff\; (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}, \qquad \text{i.e.\ } g \in M.
  \]
  This follows from \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.preimage\_basicOpen\_eq\_basicOpen} together with the characterisation ``$V \subseteq X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,h$ iff $h$ is a unit in $\Gamma(X, V)$'' (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_eq\_iff\_isUnit}, or the closely related \texttt{Scheme.basicOpen\_eq\_of\_isUnit}).

  \emph{Step 2: each $\struct{S}(D(g))$ is the single-element localization $A_g$.} For every $g \in M$, the basic-open section ring is $\struct{S}(D(g)) = A_g$, with structure map the localization of $A$ at the powers of $g$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}) supplies the instance \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$(\Gamma(S, X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g))$, i.e.\ localization of $A$ at the single element $g$.

  \emph{Step 3: directed colimit of single-element localizations is localization at the full submonoid.} The directed colimit
  \[
    \operatorname*{colim}_{g \in M}\,A_g
  \]
  of single-element localizations indexed by the (filtered) submonoid $M$ (with transition maps $A_g \to A_{gh}$ inverting the new factor $h$) is the localization $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$. This is the classical fact that localization at a multiplicative set is the directed colimit of localizations at the finitely-generated sub-monoids it contains, applied to the principal generating sub-monoids $\langle g \rangle = \{g^n\}_{n \geq 0}$ for $g \in M$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$A_g$ for each individual node, plus the universal-property characterisation via \texttt{IsLocalization.map} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic}). The exact name of the lemma ``colimit of single-element localizations under inclusion of generated submonoids equals the localization at the full submonoid'' may not yet exist in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} in this packaged form; if missing it is a Mathlib gap to be filled as part of M1.b. Candidate Mathlib home: \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Submonoid} (general filtered-submonoid colimit) or by analogy with the pattern in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.AtPrime}.

  \emph{Step 4: identify the colimit with $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} The project's $A_{\mathrm{colim}} := (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the colimit over the directed system of opens containing $f V$. Restricting to the sub-cone of opens $W \subseteq U$ does not change the colimit (any $W$ containing $f V$ can be intersected with $U$ without altering the cone since $f V \subseteq U$). By the cofinality of Step 1, this colimit equals the colimit over the cofinal subfamily $\{D(g) : g \in M\}$; by Step 2 the nodes of that subfamily are the $A_g$; by Step 3 the colimit over that subfamily is $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$. Therefore the canonical algebra map $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at $M$, i.e.\ \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.

  \emph{Mathlib-gap summary.} The cofinality lemma ``colimit over a cofinal subsystem equals colimit over the full system'' exists in Mathlib as the general \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.Final} machinery (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final}); its application to \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} may need a wrapper. Candidate Mathlib home for that wrapper: \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Presheaf.InverseImage} (new file) or \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Cofinality}. The topological analogue of the cofinality argument is recorded as Stacks Tag~02M5.

  \emph{Cross-chapter parallel.} The same cofinality argument has a parallel in M2.a's $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb{P}^{1}_{k}, A) = A(k)$ rigidity step (see \texttt{Jacobian.tex} \S~C.2), where the bridge between the algebraic-side $\Spec$ and the scheme-side $\mathbb{P}^{1}$ proceeds via a similar localization-cofinality unfold.

  The Mathlib analogist's persistent file \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md} (this iter) records the design rationale for the chosen API shape.
\end{proof}

input.old_string:
\section{Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope}
\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}

The reverse implication
\[
  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
\]
is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
input.new_string:
\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}

This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.

The reverse implication
\[
  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
\]
is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.

input.old_string: None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is out of autonomous-loop scope.
input.new_string: None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.

input.old_string:
\section{Content out of autonomous-loop scope}

The following content was developed in earlier iterations of the project and trimmed in iter-117 because the required Mathlib infrastructure is absent from the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227} and the bridge work is outside the scope of the autonomous loop. Each item lists the trimmed concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$.} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is hand-rolled work outside autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.

  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence.} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
  \[
    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
  \]
  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.

  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section.} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Out of autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.

  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity.} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
  \[
    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
  \]
  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot be expressed within the autonomous-loop's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
\end{itemize}
input.new_string:
\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}

Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.

  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
  \[
    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
  \]
  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.

  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.

  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
  \[
    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
  \]
  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
\end{itemize}

content: The file /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Let me verify the file integrity by reading the modified sections.

153	\begin{lemma}[$A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is a localization at $M$]
154	  \label{lem:appLE_isLocalization}
155	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.appLE_isLocalization}
156	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
157	  In the setting of Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE}, the canonical ring homomorphism $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M := \{g \in A : (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}\}$.
158	\end{lemma}
159	
160	\begin{proof}
161	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
162	  The proof decomposes into four steps that together identify $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ with the localization $A_M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$.
163	
164	  \emph{Step 1: cofinality of basic opens.} The directed system $\{W \subseteq S : f V \subseteq W \subseteq U\}$ of opens between $f V$ and $U$ admits as a cofinal subfamily the family of basic opens
165	  \[
166	    \{D(g) \subseteq U : g \in A,\ f V \subseteq D(g)\} \;=\; \{D(g) : g \in M\}.
167	  \]
168	  Cofinality holds because: (i) every open $W \subseteq U$ of the affine scheme $U = \Spec A$ is a union of basic opens $D(g_i)$ (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Spec}, via \texttt{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens} or equivalently \texttt{Opens.exists\_finset\_inf\_le\_basicOpen}); (ii) for any $W$ with $f V \subseteq W \subseteq U$, the topological inclusion $f V \subseteq W = \bigcup_i D(g_i)$ combined with quasi-compactness of $f V$ (the image of the quasi-compact affine $V \subseteq X$ under the continuous map $f$, contained in finitely many of the $D(g_i)$) selects a finite sub-cover $f V \subseteq D(g_{i_1}) \cup \cdots \cup D(g_{i_n})$; (iii) for the product $g := g_{i_1} \cdots g_{i_n}$ one has $D(g) = D(g_{i_1}) \cap \cdots \cap D(g_{i_n})$, hence $D(g) \subseteq D(g_{i_j}) \subseteq W$ for each $j$ and $f V \subseteq D(g)$, witnessing the cofinal refinement.
169	
170	  The bridge identity is
171	  \[
172	    f V \subseteq D(g) \;\iff\; (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}, \qquad \text{i.e.\ } g \in M.
173	  \]
174	  This follows from \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.preimage\_basicOpen\_eq\_basicOpen} together with the characterisation ``$V \subseteq X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,h$ iff $h$ is a unit in $\Gamma(X, V)$'' (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_eq\_iff\_isUnit}, or the closely related \texttt{Scheme.basicOpen\_eq\_of\_isUnit}).
175	
176	  \emph{Step 2: each $\struct{S}(D(g))$ is the single-element localization $A_g$.} For every $g \in M$, the basic-open section ring is $\struct{S}(D(g)) = A_g$, with structure map the localization of $A$ at the powers of $g$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}) supplies the instance \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$(\Gamma(S, X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g))$, i.e.\ localization of $A$ at the single element $g$.
177	
178	  \emph{Step 3: directed colimit of single-element localizations is localization at the full submonoid.} The directed colimit
179	  \[
180	    \operatorname*{colim}_{g \in M}\,A_g
181	  \]
182	  of single-element localizations indexed by the (filtered) submonoid $M$ (with transition maps $A_g \to A_{gh}$ inverting the new factor $h$) is the localization $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$. This is the classical fact that localization at a multiplicative set is the directed colimit of localizations at the finitely-generated sub-monoids it contains, applied to the principal generating sub-monoids $\langle g \rangle = \{g^n\}_{n \geq 0}$ for $g \in M$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$A_g$ for each individual node, plus the universal-property characterisation via \texttt{IsLocalization.map} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic}). The exact name of the lemma ``colimit of single-element localizations under inclusion of generated submonoids equals the localization at the full submonoid'' may not yet exist in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} in this packaged form; if missing it is a Mathlib gap to be filled as part of M1.b. Candidate Mathlib home: \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Submonoid} (general filtered-submonoid colimit) or by analogy with the pattern in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.AtPrime}.
183	
184	  \emph{Step 4: identify the colimit with $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} The project's $A_{\mathrm{colim}} := (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the colimit over the directed system of opens containing $f V$. Restricting to the sub-cone of opens $W \subseteq U$ does not change the colimit (any $W$ containing $f V$ can be intersected with $U$ without altering the cone since $f V \subseteq U$). By the cofinality of Step 1, this colimit equals the colimit over the cofinal subfamily $\{D(g) : g \in M\}$; by Step 2 the nodes of that subfamily are the $A_g$; by Step 3 the colimit over that subfamily is $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$. Therefore the canonical algebra map $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at $M$, i.e.\ \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.
185	
186	  \emph{Mathlib-gap summary.} The cofinality lemma ``colimit over a cofinal subsystem equals colimit over the full system'' exists in Mathlib as the general \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.Final} machinery (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final}); its application to \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} may need a wrapper. Candidate Mathlib home for that wrapper: \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Presheaf.InverseImage} (new file) or \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Cofinality}. The topological analogue of the cofinality argument is recorded as Stacks Tag~02M5.
187	
188	  \emph{Cross-chapter parallel.} The same cofinality argument has a parallel in M2.a's $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb{P}^{1}_{k}, A) = A(k)$ rigidity step (see \texttt{Jacobian.tex} \S~C.2), where the bridge between the algebraic-side $\Spec$ and the scheme-side $\mathbb{P}^{1}$ proceeds via a similar localization-cofinality unfold.
189	
190	  The Mathlib analogist's persistent file \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md} (this iter) records the design rationale for the chosen API shape.
191	\end{proof}
192	
193	\begin{lemma}[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
194	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
195	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
196	  \uses{}
197	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$. Then $\Omega_{L / A}$ is subsingleton (the zero $L$-module).
198	\end{lemma}
199	
200	\begin{proof}
201	  Every element of $L$ is of the form $\iota(a)\,\iota(g)^{-1}$ for some $a \in A$ and $g \in M$ (where $\iota \colon A \to L$ is the structure map). In $\Omega_{L/A}$, the universal derivation $\mathrm{d}$ over $A$ kills $\iota(a)$ and $\iota(g)$ (both are images of $A$-elements). Therefore $\mathrm{d}(\iota(a)\,\iota(g)^{-1}) = 0$ via the quotient rule, and the image of $\mathrm{d} \colon L \to \Omega_{L/A}$ is zero. Since $\Omega_{L/A}$ is generated as an $L$-module by $\mathrm{d}(L)$ (the universal property), the whole module is zero. Mathlib's \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton\_of\_isLocalization} packages this conclusion when available; otherwise the proof is a direct application of the universal property of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} as a quotient of $L \otimes_{\mathbb Z} L$ modulo the Leibniz / $A$-linearity relations.
202	\end{proof}

225	    \item \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.AffineChart} (specialisation to morphisms of schemes between affine charts).
226	  \end{itemize}
227	\end{remark}
228	
229	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
230	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
231	
232	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
233	
234	The reverse implication
235	\[
236	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
237	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
238	\]
239	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
240	
241	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
242	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
243	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
244	\end{remark}
245	
246	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
247	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
248	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
249	  \[
250	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
251	  \]
252	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
253	  \begin{itemize}
254	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
255	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
256	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
257	  \end{itemize}
258	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
259	\end{remark}
260	
261	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
262	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
263	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
264	\end{remark}
265	
266	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
267	
268	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
269	
270	\begin{itemize}
271	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
272	
273	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
274	  \[
275	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
276	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
277	  \]
278	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
279	
280	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
281	
282	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
283	  \[
284	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),

input.content:
# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
differentials-iter121

## Status
COMPLETE

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex

## Changes Made

- **Revised** proof of `lem:appLE_isLocalization` (the M1.b proof skeleton). The previous one-paragraph proof referenced "a cofinality-of-directed-colimit lemma that may need to be supplied" without a proof shape. The new proof has a concrete four-step decomposition:
  - *Step 1 — Cofinality of basic opens.* Quasi-compactness of `f V` selects a finite sub-cover among `{D(g_i)}`; product `g = ∏ g_i` gives `D(g) = ⋂ D(g_i) ⊆ W` with `f V ⊆ D(g)`. Named Mathlib pieces: `PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens` (or `Opens.exists_finset_inf_le_basicOpen`). Bridge identity `f V ⊆ D(g) ⟺ appLE(g) ∈ B^×` cites `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.preimage_basicOpen_eq_basicOpen` + `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.basicOpen_eq_iff_isUnit`.
  - *Step 2 — Single-element localization.* Each `O_S(D(g)) = A_g` via `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen [verified]`.
  - *Step 3 — Colimit of single-element localizations.* `colim_{g ∈ M} A_g = Localization M A`. Names the Mathlib gap explicitly (lemma "colimit over inclusion of generated submonoids = localization at full submonoid" may need supplying); flags candidate home `Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Submonoid`.
  - *Step 4 — Assemble.* The presheaf-side `A_colim` is the directed colimit over the cofinal subsystem, hence equals `Localization M A` via `IsLocalization M A_colim`.
  - *Mathlib-gap summary* names `CategoryTheory.Functor.Final` as the cofinality machinery and `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Presheaf.InverseImage` / `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Cofinality` as wrapper homes.
  - Adds a *Cross-chapter parallel* sentence pointing at `Jacobian.tex § C.2` (M2.a `Hom(ℙ¹_k, A) = A(k)`) — one sentence only, as directed.
  - Stacks Tag 02M5 cited as the topological analogue.

- **Revised** `\uses{...}` on `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. Was `\uses{lem:appLE_isLocalization}` (logically backwards: kernel of localization is independent of appLE setup). Now `\uses{}` (empty, per directive). The opposite direction `\uses{lem:appLE_isLocalization}` correctly remains on `thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE` (Step M1.b consumer).

- **Revised** Section heading "Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope" → "Converse direction (milestone M4)" (label `sec:converse-out-of-scope` kept intact so the two existing `\ref` sites in the chapter intro and § "Smoothness criterion" continue to resolve). Added a leading paragraph: "This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone M4 (see `STRATEGY.md`). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis `Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent A B)` ..." per the directive's verbatim phrasing.

- **Revised** in-body "is out of autonomous-loop scope" in `rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses` → "is the content of milestone M4."

- **Revised** Section heading "Content out of autonomous-loop scope" → "Content scheduled for later milestones". Added a leading paragraph framing the roadmap progression M1 → M2/M3 → M4 → M5+, and tagged each of the four bullets with an explicit milestone number: M5 (sheaf condition), M6 (cotangent exact sequence), M7 (cotangent space at section), M8 (Serre-duality genus identity). The "outside autonomous-loop scope" and "Out of autonomous-loop scope" phrases in those bullets were rewritten to "the deferred work for this milestone" / removed entirely; the "cannot be expressed within the autonomous-loop's Mathlib snapshot" phrase changed to "cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot."

## Cross-references introduced

- `\uses{}` (empty) on `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton` — replaces an incorrect backwards dependency. No new lookups needed.
- Cross-chapter `\S~C.2` reference to `Jacobian.tex` from the `lem:appLE_isLocalization` proof. This is intentional one-sentence orientation, not a `\uses{...}` link (the cross-chapter parallel is informational, not a logical dependency).
- `\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}` from the new roadmap paragraph in "Content scheduled for later milestones" — resolves to the renamed M4 section in the same file.

## Macros needed
- None. The new proof uses only macros already in use in this chapter (`\struct`, `\Spec`, standard math env macros).

## Reference-retriever dispatches
- None. All Mathlib lemma names cited are either already named in the prior version of this chapter or are the directive's verbatim suggestions; no external textbook material was needed beyond Stacks Tag 02M5 (cited only as an analogue) and Hartshorne (already in `summary.md`'s ambient context).

## Notes for Plan Agent

- The "out of autonomous-loop scope" framing appeared at six occurrences total in the original chapter (directive said "four times"); I updated all six for consistency. Two of them were section headings (now renamed), one was an inline phrase in `rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses` (now "is the content of milestone M4"), and three were in the tail-section bullets (rewritten in place). If the reviewer prefers a stricter four-edit reading, the body-level rewrites can be partially reverted — but the resulting chapter would be inconsistent with the two new section headings, so I judged six-of-six was the better outcome.
- The intro paragraph (line 6) and the Section-2 paragraph (line ~46) both contain prose phrases like "is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}". Since the directive said only to update the section headings (and not the cross-reference label), the body text remains accurate after the heading rename — those `\ref`s resolve to "Section X" which now reads "Converse direction (milestone M4)" in the rendered output. No edit was needed there. If the reviewer wants the label itself renamed (to e.g. `sec:converse-m4`), a second pass would update those two `\ref` sites plus the label.
- The M1.b proof skeleton names `IsLocalization.Away g A_g` (Step 2) and a candidate "colimit of single-element localizations = localization at submonoid" lemma (Step 3). The Step 3 lemma's exact Mathlib name in snapshot `b80f227` is not verified — this is flagged explicitly in the proof as a potential Mathlib gap, with `Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Submonoid` named as the candidate home. The next iteration's prover lane on M1.b should verify whether this lemma exists or whether the project needs to supply it.
- Empty `\uses{}` syntax: I followed the directive literally. Some `leanblueprint` versions treat `\uses{}` and an omitted `\uses` identically; if the rendering pass complains, the fix is to delete the line entirely (the underlying dependency graph is the same).

## Strategy-modifying findings

None. The Step 3 Mathlib-gap surfaced in M1.b is consistent with what `STRATEGY.md` already anticipates ("Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1").
